blocks.speaker: Pastor
blocks.content: Kit, do you take Daisy May Jones to be your lawfully wedded wife — to have and to hold, in joy and in sorrow, in sickness and in health, for richer and for poorer, as long as you both shall live?

blocks.speaker: Pastor
blocks.content: Daisy, do you take Kit Jenkins to be your lawfully wedded husband — to have and to hold, in joy and in sorrow, in sickness and in health, for richer and for poorer, as long as you both shall live?
She looked at him like she had been waiting a long time to say it.
Eli handed them over. His hands were steady. His eyes were not.

blocks.speaker: Pastor
blocks.content: Kit — place the ring on her hand and repeat after me. With this ring, I thee wed.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: With this ring — I thee wed.
His hands were shaking. He got it on.

blocks.speaker: Pastor
blocks.content: Daisy — place the ring on his hand and repeat after me. With this ring, I thee wed.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: With this ring — I thee wed.
Pastor Hayes looked at them both for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Pastor
blocks.content: Now. You have both prepared some words of your own. This is that moment. Kit — whenever you are ready.
